payload.title: Tier 1 — New frontend extensions (Clippy-proof bug classes)
payload.description:
Spend the frontend/lowering budget on NEW bug classes that a linter structurally cannot catch, in the spirit of named-args: cheap, source-to-source, high bug-caught-per-unit-complexity.

Design discipline (the effects lesson): the effect keyword was the most ambitious type-system extension and it was REMOVED. The bar for anything here: kill a real, observed agent bug class with lowering-level machinery only. No new type system, no solver, no dataflow. If a feature starts wanting those, cut it.

Stories are eval-gated where noted: don't build until a run shows agents actually shipping that bug.
